{{Quote|Traditional Jedi robes might look fairly plain, but that was the point. The Jedi were more focused on serving the will of the Force than on the material comforts of the physical world.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SotJ" />}}
'''Jedi apparel''' was the clothing worn by [[Jedi]] of the [[Jedi Order]]. The most traditional example were the [[monk]]-like [[Clothing|garments]] and [[robe]]s known simply as '''Jedi robes'''. Although they were free to choose their clothing from the [[time]] they became [[Padawan]]s, most [[Jedi]] adopted the garments traditionally associated with the Order, with notable exceptions like [[Aayla Secura]] and [[Ahsoka Tano]]. Jedi apparel varied in [[color]], but were predominantly tan or brown. During the [[Republic Era|final years]] of the [[Galactic Republic]], the [[Jedi Master]]s were unsettled by the dark Jedi robes worn by [[Anakin Skywalker]].

[[Armor]] was used to complement traditional Jedi robes in times of war; the [[Clone Wars]] saw the development of the [[Jedi Commander armor]], a combination of Jedi garments and modified [[clone trooper armor]]. Jedi robes were still worn by some surviving members of the Order after the [[Great Jedi Purge]] of [[Order 66]]. With the [[New Republic Era|end]] of the [[Galactic Empire]]'s [[Imperial Era|reign]], [[Luke Skywalker]] [[Jedi training|trained]] a new generation of [[Padawan|Jedi apprentices]] who wore Jedi robes. After the [[Destruction of the Jedi Temple|destruction]] of his [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker|temple]], Skywalker kept a set of traditional robes that he intended to wear for his final ritual as a Jedi.

==Description==
===Jedi apparel===
{{Quote|While some Jedi incorporated traditional elements from their homeworld's culture into their clothing, most Jedi robes were a basic tan or brown.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SotJ" />}}
[[File:Jedi_Knight_RotS.png|thumb|left|180px|Jedi robes could withstand difficult weather conditions, including rain and sandstorms.]]
[[Jedi]] tradition emphasized the worship of [[the Force]] and complete devotion to its ways. As such, members of the [[Jedi Order]] were less invested in their appearance and therefore typically [[Clothing|clothed]] in plain [[robe]]s.<ref name="SotJ" /> Although the Jedi had no set [[uniform]]s,<ref name="Battlefront II" /> Jedi apparel<ref name="EyeofDarkness">''[[The High Republic: The Eye of Darkness]]''</ref> tended to follow a pattern,<ref name="SotJ" /> consisting of an undertunic, an overtunic, a [[cloak]] with wide sleeves and a deep hood that provided the wearer with anonymity,<ref name="Battlefront II" /> loose-fitting trousers, a tabard, a broad sash tied around the waist<ref name="Episode I" /> with a [[belt]],<ref name="SotJ" /> and tall [[boot]]s. Those pieces of clothing and footwear generally varied in [[Color|light earth tones]],<ref name="Episode I" /> such as tan and brown,<ref name="SotJ" /> though several Jedi were known for wearing darker versions.<ref name="Episode III">{{Film|III}}</ref> In the [[eye]]s of some, like the [[Kalleran]] scoundrel [[Janus Kasmir]], Jedi robes made their wearers look similar to [[monk]]s.<ref name="Kanan 2">[[Kanan 2|''Kanan'' 2]]</ref>

Having adopted a simple yet strict life style, most Jedi chose to wear robes in a traditional earth-tone. The clothes were warm and comfortable,<ref name="VE">''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and despite their constrictive appearance,<ref name="Thrawn Alliances" /> they allowed for greater freedom of movement.<ref name="VE" /> In addition to not hindering the Jedi's range of motion, the robes created a "swirling" effect that served to distract an opponent in combat.<ref name="Thrawn Alliances" /> Aside from their combat use, Jedi robes provided protection in severe weather conditions, making them particularly useful on [[Kamino]] or [[desert]]-covered planets.<ref name="Battlefront II" /> The Jedi Order also permitted members to wear robes from their [[homeworld]], such as [[Barriss Offee]]'s hybrid [[Mirialan]]-[[Padawan]] outfit<ref name="VE" /> or the elegant garments worn by the [[Noble|high-born]] [[Dooku]]<ref name="Battlefront II" /> of [[House Serenno]].<ref name="Dooku: Jedi Lost">''[[Dooku: Jedi Lost]]''</ref>

Traditional Jedi robes were worn by both [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]]<ref name="Battlefront II" /> and [[Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="TLJ VD">''[[Star Wars: The Last Jedi: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> Yoda utilized his cloak's hood for the purpose of disguise,<ref name="Battlefront II" /> and Skywalker reserved his traditional garments for his final rite as a Jedi.<ref name="TLJ VD" />

===Jedi armor===
{{Quote|Because of the Jedi's dangerous position on the front lines, traditional robes weren't always enough to protect members of the Order from the constant barrage of attacks unleashed by the Separatists' droids. To meet this threat, many Jedi incorporated armor—usually modified from standard-issue clone trooper gear—into their ceremonial garb.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SotJ" />}}
[[File:FiveStar-General-Kenobi.png|thumb|right|250px|In wartime some Jedi wore armor plates over their traditional robes.]]
[[Jedi Commander armor]] was a type of personal body [[armor]] worn by some members of the Jedi Order in wartime.<ref name="Engines of War">{{FantasyFlightGames|url=en/news/2018/11/14/the-engines-of-war|text=The Engines of War}}</ref> Developed at 5,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard|credits]]<ref name="Rise">''[[Rise of the Separatists]]''</ref> and given out to Jedi after the [[Jedi Military Integration Act]] was passed,<ref name="Brotherhood">[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']]</ref> Jedi [[Commander]] armor was a modified version of the [[clone trooper armor]]. It was developed during the [[Clone Wars]] and utilized by the [[Jedi General]]s of the [[Grand Army of the Republic]],<ref name="Engines of War" /> who fought alongside the [[clone trooper]]s on numerous battlefronts across [[the galaxy]].<ref name="clonetroopers">{{Databank|clone-troopers|Clone Troopers}}</ref> Notwithstanding the [[Force power]]s and [[lightsaber combat]] skills of the Jedi, they were vulnerable to [[blaster]] fire and as such wore armor over their ceremonial robes during the conflict against the [[Separatist Droid Army]],<ref name="SotJ" /> which was composed of billions of [[battle droid]]s.<ref name="Battlefront II" />

Another type of armor used by the Jedi was the [[Jedi Reconnaissance Armor]]. Costing 4,000 credits, it was created during the [[Cloning|Clone]] Wars to assuage the clone [[trooper]]s' fears for the safety of their [[general]]s. The Jedi therefore utilized a type of lightweight armor that offered some defense against blasters but without hindering their range of motion.<ref name="Collapse">''[[Collapse of the Republic]]''</ref>

==History==
===The age of the Jedi===
====Overview====
[[File:MaceLuminaraObiWanOppo-Voices.png|thumb|left|250px|In the Jedi Order attire varied according to the individual's preference.]]
While there were changes in overall robe styles across Jedi history,<ref name="Ven Path">''[[The High Republic: Path of Vengeance]]''</ref> the Jedi Order did not enforce a uniform dress code; as such, the Jedi could wear the attire of their choice after they achieved the status of Padawan,<ref name="Battlefront II" /> with even some Padawans adopting clothing that did not match the typical apparel of the Order.<ref name="Weapons Factory">{{TCW|Weapons Factory}}</ref> Nonetheless, most Jedi decided to wear the traditional Jedi robes, with only a small number breaking from the traditional formula<ref name="Galactic Gear Anakin">{{Topps|set=2024 Topps Star Wars Chrome|subset=Galactic Gear|cardname=Anakin Skywalker|link=https://www.ebay.com/itm/296667695729}}</ref> that acted as a prescribed dress code.<ref name="Galactic Gear Ahsoka">{{Topps|set=2024 Topps Star Wars Chrome|subset=Galactic Gear|cardname=Ahsoka Tano|link=https://www.ebay.com/itm/326253274385}}</ref>

Some species among the ranks of the Jedi needed to change the basic appearance to suit their needs. [[Kel Dor]] Jedi, such as Master [[Plo Koon]], wore [[goggles]] and an [[antiox breath mask]]<ref name="Episode III" /> to keep themselves safe when off of their species homeworld of [[Dorin]].<ref name="Aliens of the Galaxy">''[[Star Wars: Aliens of the Galaxy]]''</ref> Being a [[hair]]-covered species, a [[Wookiee]] Jedi did not necessary need to wear Jedi robes; the Padawan [[Gungi]] wore nothing but a sash, a belt, and a bandolier.<ref name="TCW:TG">{{TCW|The Gathering (episode)}}</ref> However, other Wookiee Jedi—such as [[Burryaga Agaburry]], who had both mission and Temple attire like other [[High Republic Era]]-Jedi,<ref name="TGJR" /> and [[Arkoff]]—had worn the Order's apparel.<ref name="Edge of Balance 1">[[The High Republic: The Edge of Balance, Vol. 1|''The High Republic: The Edge of Balance'', Vol. 1]]</ref>

====High Republic Era====
[[File:ACoruscantSolstice-LifeDayTreasury.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Jedi of the High Republic Era maintained a set of white and gold Temple robes.]]
During the High Republic Era, Jedi wore brown robes with a golden trim<ref name="TGJR">''[[The High Republic: The Great Jedi Rescue]]''</ref> identified as [[mission robes]].<ref name="Rising Storm">''[[The High Republic: The Rising Storm]]''</ref> They also had access to white and gold colored garments<ref name="TGJR" /> known as [[Temple robes]].<ref name="Rising Storm" /> A fine ceremonial clothing,<ref name="Chronicles" /> Temple robes saw use at formal events like the [[dedication of the Starlight Beacon]].<ref name="TGJR" /> [[Kufa]] of the [[Ro family]] mocked the golden robes as overly showy, dismissing the Jedi as people who did not understand the Force as per her<ref name="Rising Storm" /> warped understanding of the Force<ref name="Path of Deceit">''[[The High Republic: Path of Deceit]]''</ref> passed down from<ref name="Rising Storm" /> the [[Path of the Open Hand]] cult. In order to sneak into the [[Temple of the Kyber]]<ref name="Ven Path" /> in [[382 BBY]],<ref name="Ven Death">''[[Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia]]'' dates the events of ''[[The High Republic: Path of Vengeance]]'' to [[382 BBY]].</ref> Path member [[Yana Ro]] was provided with a set of slightly outdated robes by her reluctant ally, the corrupt [[chief archivist]] [[Feric Oranalli]]. While Ro mockingly thought that Oranalli's description of the apparel as outdated made it seem like the Jedi cared about always staying slightly ahead of fashion trends, the robes worked just fine as a way to sneak in.<ref name="Ven Path" />

During the High Republic Era, two Jedi, [[Barash Silvain]] and [[Porter Engle]], developed a bond akin to that of siblings. Despite working closely together as they traveled throughout the galaxy,<ref name="The Blade 1">[[The High Republic — The Blade 1|''The High Republic — The Blade'' 1]]</ref> they adopted contrasting clothing in line with their opposing attitudes; Engle wore a set of dark robes,<ref name="Chronicles">''[[Star Wars: The High Republic: Chronicles of the Jedi]]''</ref> although some of his central robes and his sleeves were lighter,<ref name="The Blade 1" /> while Silvain adopted radiantly white apparel.<ref name="Chronicles" /> Much of her outer robes<ref name="The Blade 4">[[The High Republic — The Blade 4|''The High Republic — The Blade'' 4]]</ref> and her hood were tan.<ref name="The Blade 1" /> After Silvain stepped away from active Jedi duty<ref name="The Blade 4" /> in what would become known as the [[Barash Vow]],<ref name="Chronicles" /> Engle reported his sister's actions to the [[Jedi High Council]] while wearing a set of cream-colored robes.<ref name="The Blade 4" />

During the High Republic Era, one Jedi [[Wayseeker]]—a position amongst the Order in which a Jedi would operate outside the confines of the [[Jedi High Council]]'s orders<ref name="Into the Dark">''[[The High Republic: Into the Dark]]''</ref>—was [[Orla Jareni]], who wore a set of white robes at all times, which meant she stood out amongst brown-robed Jedi during missions where she found herself with others.<ref name="THR 8">[[The High Republic (2021) 8|''The High Republic'' (2021) 8]]</ref> Jedi Padawan [[Reath Silas]] found her entire look, in part due to her outfit and general [[Umbaran]] appearance, to be as individual as her decisions, with her blank robes appearing so clean that her also white skin seemingly had more color. Back during her days as a Padawan,<ref name="Into the Dark" /> she had worn a set of white robes during the [[Eiram–E'ronoh crisis]], a mission during which her then-fellow Padawan [[Cohmac Vitus]] also wore white robes.<ref name="A Step Into">{{HighRepublicShow|4y6eZpmHCe4|A Step Into the Dark, Art of The High Republic, and More!|sw_url=video/a-step-into-the-dark-art-of-the-high-republic-and-more}}</ref> As a Jedi Master, Vitus wore<ref name="Into the Dark" /> the brown robes with a golden trim<ref name="THR 8" /><ref name="Into the Dark art">{{ArtStation|username=5health|url=artwork/49eK0n|name=5health|text=Official art for the Japanese edition of "Into the Dark"}}</ref> more commonly seen as era's mission attire.<ref name="TGJR" /><ref name="THR 8" />

[[File:JediOnMulita-2021THR8.png|thumb|left|300px|Jedi of the High Republic in their mission attire robes, with Orla Jareni standing out in her white attire and OrbaLin wearing an EVA suit.]]
One Jedi in special attire was [[OrbaLin]], who, as a<ref name="THR 15">[[The High Republic (2021) 15|''The High Republic'' (2021) 15]]</ref> [[blob]]-based<ref name="Solo Official Guide">''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide]]''</ref> [[Ugor]], wore an [[environment suit]]<ref name="Rising Storm" /> with a dome-like top<ref name="THR 15" /> to maintain a humanoid appearance.<ref name="Rising Storm" /> His suit was white and gold in color as if<ref name="THR 15" /> it was a set of Temple robes.<ref name="TGJR" /> OrbaLin was the first member of his species to ever join the Jedi Order.<ref name="Rising Storm" /> After losing his EVA suit, OrbaLin was able to continue his service as a Jedi but in his natural blob state.<ref name="THR 2023 4">[[The High Republic (2023) 4|''The High Republic'' (2023) 4]]</ref>

At some point during the [[Nihil conflict|conflict]] between the [[Nihil]] [[Pirate|marauders]] and Jedi-backed Republic, a [[Battle on Gallimere|battle]] erupted on [[Gallimere]], thanks to which a [[Unidentified human (Gallimere)|human citizen]] found a fallen lightsaber. Deciding that the people of Gallimere needed a hero, the human impersonated a Jedi by donning robes and wielding the lightsaber against the Nihil, but found they had underestimated the Nihils' numbers. Sometime after the [[Deployment of the stormseeds|establishment]] of the [[Nihil]] [[Pirate|marauders']] [[Occlusion Zone]]<ref name="All the Republic">{{StoryCite|story=All the Republic|book=Revelations (2023) 1}}</ref> with the [[stormwall]] as its border,<ref name="Eye of Darkness">''[[The High Republic: The Eye of Darkness]]''</ref> which Gallimere sat outside of but bordered upon, Jedi Master [[Keeve Trennis]] arrived on Gallimere after hearing rumors of a lone Jedi waging a campaign against the Nihil. Discovering [[Skirmish on Gallimere|another Nihil raid on Gallimere]], Trennis discovered the robe-wearing and lightsaber-wielding human, who confessed they were not a Jedi but wanted to give the local people hope. The individual helped Trennis defeat the Nihil force and was assured by the Jedi Master that all they needed to serve the light side of the Force was hope.<ref name="All the Republic" />

====Late Republic Era====
{{Quote|Your sleeves are cut to allow exaggerated movements of your arms. Likewise, the long tunic, which appears as if it would impede your movement, also allows freedom of motion. Of equal importance is the fact that in combat its swirling motion will distract an opponent's eyes.|Mitth'raw'nuruodo, to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="Thrawn Alliances" />}}
[[File:Communication_center.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Jedi had largely adopted brown and tan colored robes by the late Republic Era, but exceptions, such as the outfit worn by Aayla Secura (far left), still existed.]]
As the Jedi Order carried on during the Republic Era,<ref name="Episode III" /> the white Temple Robes disappeared from the Order while the mission robe became increasingly common place,<ref name="Acolyte Architect">{{InsiderCite|226|Leslye Headland: Acolyte & Architect}}</ref> seeing use on both missions and within temples.<ref name="Episode III" /> Even then, the golden trim seen within the High Republic Era mission robe<ref name="TGJR" /> vanished from an overall brown and tan robe design.<ref name="Episode III" /> Nonetheless, a strict "dress code" still did not outright exist<ref name="Battlefront II" /> even though most Jedi obeyed tradition. Among the more individualistic Jedi, who strayed from tradition to showcase more their individual traits,<ref name="Galactic Gear Anakin" /> was the [[Twi'lek]] [[Aayla Secura]], who wore fitted clothes that allowed complete freedom of movement and left her midriff exposed.<ref name="Episode III" /> Another Twi'lek Jedi, [[Finn Ertay]], wore a long-sleeved, high-necked full-length dress that began to flare below the waist.<ref name="Revival">{{TCW|Revival (episode)}}</ref> [[Mirialan]] Jedi Master [[Luminara Unduli]] wore a set of dark robes and a headdress that set her apart from other Jedi,<ref name="Episode III" /> with her apprentice, the fellow Mirialan [[Barriss Offee]], donning her own unique set of blue robes.<ref name="Weapons Factory">{{TCW|Weapons Factory}}</ref>

With the passage of the Jedi Military Integration Act in the early weeks of the Clone Wars, the Order was officially provided military positions in the [[Republic Military]] and, with that formal authority, were granted pieces of body armor<ref name="Brotherhood" /> created out of clone trooper armor. The gear was identified as Jedi Commander armor.<ref name="Engines of War" /> Jedi Knight [[Anakin Skywalker]] and Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] were provided with armor after the passage of the act,<ref name="Brotherhood" /> but they did not immediately adopt the gear.<ref name="HS 5">[[Hyperspace Stories 5|''Hyperspace Stories'' 5]]</ref> In time, however, Skywalker and Kenobi would both begin wearing armor as part of their outfits;<ref name="TCWfilm">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]]</ref> Skywalker had made the shift by the time of the [[Battle of Arantara]],<ref name="501 Plus One">{{StoryCite|story=501 Plus One|book=Age of Republic Special 1}}</ref> while Kenobi had taken on the gear by the time of the [[mission on Krystar]].<ref name="Battle Tales 3">[[Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales 3|''Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales'' 3]]</ref>

[[File:JediTemplehangar-ROTS.png|thumb|left|300px|Anakin Skywalker wore darker robes than the other members of the Jedi Order.]]
During the Clone Wars, Skywalker wore a series of dark robes,<ref name="Episode III" /><ref name="TCWfilm" /><ref name="Heroes">{{TCW|Heroes on Both Sides}}</ref> which concerned older members of the Jedi Order.<ref name="Battlefront II" /> In his robes completed by Jedi armor, Skywalker wore a red and blue fitted tunic with dark armor plates attached to it, including a<ref name="TCWfilm" /> heavy, arm-length glove<ref name="Galactic Gear Anakin" /> over his cybernetic right hand. His left hand featured a vambrace completed with a comlink.<ref name="TCWfilm" /> Later, he adopted an outfit without clone armor, although he keep maintain the comlink-equipped vambrace on his left hand and the long glove. His new robes were of a dark brown/maroon color, also featuring even darker outer robe portions,<ref name="Heroes" /> although he did shift back to his prior outfit at the time of the [[mission to Batuu]] and [[mission to Mokivj]].<ref name="Alliances 2">[[Thrawn: Alliances 2|''Thrawn: Alliances'' 2]]</ref> During that operation, the [[Chiss]] [[commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] noted the practical applications of the Jedi robes worn by Skywalker.<ref name="Thrawn Alliances" /> In time, Skywalker would shift to even darker robes<ref name="Episode III" /> that featured a leather vest<ref name="Galactic Gear Anakin" /> but lacked any form of armor. He retained his cybernetic arm covering but left his left hand ungloved.<ref name="Episode III" />

After Kenobi had taken to wearing protective armor as a Jedi General, his gear was fitted over tan Jedi robes.<ref name="TCWfilm" /> Its design was derived from that of the clone troopers who served in his [[212th Attack Battalion]].<ref name="Battlefront II" /> In time, however, he shifted away from wearing so much armor and only donned two forearm pieces adorned with the symbol of the [[Open Circle Fleet]].<ref name="ARC Troopers">{{TCW|ARC Troopers (episode)}}</ref> He later dropped armor altogether.<ref name="Episode III" /> The [[Kiffar]] Jedi [[Quinlan Vos]] favored sleeveless tunics,<ref name="Hunt for Ziro">{{TCW|Hunt for Ziro}}</ref> and occasionally donned a black [[leather]] outfit.<ref name="Dark Disciple">''[[Dark Disciple]]''</ref> 

[[File:AhsokaTanoPadawan-TLC.png|thumb|right|250px|Ahsoka Tano in her later adopted outfit]]
The [[Togruta]] [[Ahsoka Tano]] served on the frontline of the Clone Wars as Skywalker's Padawan learner.<ref name="TCWfilm" /> Much like Skywalker, she was an example of a Jedi who did not bind herself to<ref name="Galactic Gear Ahsoka" /> the traditional<ref name="Battlefront II" /> Jedi 'dress code.' Instead preferring a sportier, more youthful clothing style,<ref name="Galactic Gear Ahsoka" /> she first donned a bandeau top and skirt, which she had worn since before she met Skywalker.<ref name="TCWfilm" /> By the time of her ensuing apprenticeship, she also owned a set of clothing that covered much more of her body, all without compromising her freedom of movement.<ref name="Practice Makes Perfect>{{TOTJ|Practice Makes Perfect (episode)}}</ref> She later fully adopted an outfit which covered more of her skin<ref name="Heroes" /> while still allowing great freedom of movement.<ref name="Friends and Enemies">{{TCW|Friends and Enemies}}</ref> She wore that outfit for the remainder of her Jedi service,<ref name="TWJ">{{TCW|The Wrong Jedi}}</ref> only changing her day-to-day attire after she left the Order<ref name="Gone with a Trace">{{TCW|Gone with a Trace}}</ref> upon losing faith in the organization.<ref name="TWJ" />

===The fall of the Jedi===
{{Quote|You can't walk around dressed like a monk while clones are on the hunt for Jedi.|Janus Kasmir, to Caleb Dume|<ref name="Kanan 2" />}}
[[File:Anakin_Skywalker_CotR.png|thumb|left|200px|Darth Vader prior to his reconstruction retained the Jedi robes that he wore as Anakin Skywalker.]]
By the end of the Clone Wars, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Sidious]] had [[Duel in Palpatine's office|succeeded]] in converting Anakin Skywalker to the [[dark side of the Force]]. They hurriedly moved to restore [[Sith]] rule to [[the galaxy]] by destroying the Jedi Order. As a result, the newly-anointed [[Sith apprentice]] [[Darth]] Vader retained the dark robes that he wore as a [[Jedi Knight]]. During the [[Operation: Knightfall|siege of the Jedi Temple]], Vader discovered a group of [[Jedi Initiate]]s hiding in the [[Jedi Council Chamber]]. Still dressed in his Jedi garments, he was mistook for a Jedi by [[Unidentified Jedi youngling|one]] of the [[youngling]]s, who believed that Skywalker had come to help them.<ref name="Episode III" />

As part of the attack on the Jedi Temple and then gatekeeping it afterward,<ref name="Kit Reveal">{{EA|url=games/starwars/galaxy-of-heroes/news/kit-reveal-disguised-clone-trooper|text=Kit Reveal: Disguised Clone Trooper}}</ref> several clone troopers<ref name="French Novel">''[[Revenge of the Sith - The Film Novel]]''</ref>—identified as [[Disguised Clone Trooper]]s<ref name="Kit Reveal" />—donned Jedi robes to lure unsuspecting Jedi survivors towards the building.<ref name="French Novel" /> Meanwhile, Vader continued to wear Jedi robes during his [[Mission to Mustafar|mission]] to [[Volcano|volcanic]] world of [[Mustafar]], where he executed the [[Executive Separatist Council|leadership]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on behalf of his [[Sith Master]]. However, his [[Duel on Mustafar|duel]] with Obi-Wan Kenobi across the [[magma]]-covered plains of Mustafar led to Vader's immolation in which his Jedi attire was [[Fire|burned]] and consequently replaced by a [[Darth Vader's armor|black suit of armor]].<ref name="Episode III" />

With the [[Galactic Republic]] [[Proclamation of the New Order|supplanted]] by the [[Galactic Empire]], the surviving Jedi scattered into [[Exile (punishment)|exile]] to escape the [[Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="Episode III" /> While some Jedi such as Yoda,<ref name="Episode V">{{Film|V}}</ref> Kenobi,<ref name="Episode IV">{{Film|IV}}</ref> and [[Ferren Barr]] continued to wear Jedi robes during the [[Imperial Era]],<ref name="Dark Lord 13">[[Darth Vader (2017) 13|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 13]]</ref> [[Cal Kestis]]<ref name="Jedi: Fallen Order">''[[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]''</ref> and [[Kanan Jarrus|Caleb Dume]]<ref name="Kanan 2" /> had adopted non-Jedi attire—in Kestis' case, the [[Rigger (occupation)|work clothes]] of the [[Scrapper Guild]] on [[Bracca]]—while hiding as fugitives under [[Order 66]].<ref name="Jedi: Fallen Order" />

===The new generation of Jedi===
{{Quote|The kid looks like trash. An actual pile of garbage. Find him some clothes. I mean… we're the ''Knights of Ren.'' We have a reputation.|Ren, referring to Ben Solo's Jedi robes|<ref name="Kylo 3">[[The Rise of Kylo Ren 3|''The Rise of Kylo Ren'' 3]]</ref>}}
[[File:LukeTeachingJedi-TheRiseOfKyloRen3.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Luke Skywalker and his apprentices wore the distinctive clothes of the Jedi Order.]]
Anakin Skywalker's [[Skywalker family|son]], Luke Skywalker, became the last Jedi after the [[death]] of his mentor Yoda. Taking the title of Jedi Master, he [[Jedi training|trained]] multiple apprentices at once, including his nephew [[Ben Solo]]. The members of the new generation of Jedi were clothed in Jedi garments since the early [[Standard day|days]] of their apprenticeship,<ref name="Kylo 3" /> as was Skywalker who wore woven Jedi robes which included a traditional Jedi belt buckle.<ref name="TROS VD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>

In the wake of the [[Destruction of the Jedi Temple|destruction]] of [[Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker|Luke Skywalker's Jedi temple]], Solo sought to join the [[Knights of Ren]]. Due to [[Ren (human)|Ren's]] disapproval of Solo's Jedi attire, the former Padawan replaced his tattered robes with darker garments that were reminiscent of the Knights' battle gear.<ref name="Kylo 3" />

Skywalker fled to the Jedi Order's ancestral homeworld of [[Ahch-To]], where he stored various Jedi relics as well as a set of traditional Jedi robes that he only intended to wear for the completion of his last rite as a Jedi.<ref name="TLJ VD" /> Disillusioned by the Order's past defeats and his nephew's transformation into Kylo Ren, Skywalker wore his traditional robes when he attempted to burn a [[uneti tree]] that functioned as a [[Tree library|repository]] for the [[Sacred Jedi texts|last traces]] of the Jedi [[religion]].<ref name="Episode VIII">{{Film|VIII}}</ref>

===The last of the Jedi===
[[File:Rey_SoH.png|thumb|left|200px|Rey wore a set of Jedi training robes following her apprenticeship on Ahch-To.]]
In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Starkiller Base]], Luke Skywalker was discovered hiding on Ahch-To by [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]], a [[scavenger]] from the planet [[Jakku]]. Skywalker was wearing his traditional Jedi robes at the time of his first encounter with Rey,<ref name="Episode VIII" /> whose outfit included "a loose openweave tabard in Jedi tradition."<ref name="TROS VD" /> At the end of her time on Ahch-To, Rey changed her clothes to a set of<ref name="Episode VIII" /> Jedi training<ref name="Battlefront II" /> garments before confronting Kylo Ren in person onboard the ''[[Supremacy]]''.<ref name="Episode VIII" /> As with her previous attire,<ref name="TROS VD" /> the training outfit was influenced by Jedi tradition, featuring "a loose open weave tabard."<ref name="TLJ VD" />

By the time of the [[Battle of Crait]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> Skywalker had abandoned his plans to end the Jedi Order.<ref name="SotJ" /> Although he physically remained on Ahch-To, he was able to manifest a [[Force projection|projection]] himself on [[Crait]], transmitting his presence across the [[galaxy]] through the Force.<ref name="Episode VIII" /> The appearance of Skywalker clad in Jedi robes surprised his [[twin]] sister, General [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]],<ref name="Luke DB">{{Databank|luke-skywalker|Luke Skywalker}}</ref> whose [[Resistance]] he intended to save from the [[First Order]] and its new ruler, [[Supreme Leader]] Kylo Ren.<ref name="Episode VIII" />

[[File:Luke_Skywalker_on_Crait.png|thumb|right|250px|An illusion of Luke Skywalker clad in Jedi robes appeared on Crait during a battle between the First Order and the Resistance.]]
Skywalker's illusion reflected a younger version of himself, wearing the same Jedi robes that he had during his time as Ben Solo's Jedi Master. The sight of his former master triggered Ren's rage, preventing him from discerning the inconsistencies in Skywalker's appearance.<ref name="TROS VD" /> Ren consequently failed to destroy the remnants of the Resistance as he originally intended, but the effort of projecting himself across [[Realspace|space]] and time led to Skywalker's death. As a result, his body vanished along with his Jedi garments, leaving only the outer robe after he became [[Force spirit|one with the Force]].<ref name="Episode VIII" />

Rey, having committed to learning the Jedi arts, took to wearing white garments that were patterned on the clothes that she wore during her time as a scavenger. In addition to honoring her origins on Jakku,<ref name="Battlefront II" /> Rey's chosen attire included a lightweight synthetic [[cape]]let that reflected the [[Alderaanian]] heritage of her mentor, [[Princess]] Leia Organa of [[Alderaan]]. She also utilized trousers with padded knees while engaging in the [[Ajan Kloss training course|athletic exercises]] of her Jedi training on the planet [[Ajan Kloss]].<ref name="TROS VD" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|When she comes back, will she be a Jedi like in the stories? Brown robes, little rat tail?|Rose Tico, to Finn about Rey|<ref name="TLJ EE">''[[Star Wars: The Last Jedi: Expanded Edition]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Tony-foti-starwars-forcecollector-fullwrap-art.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Karr Nuq Sin's inheritance included a set of Jedi robes that belonged to his great-grandfather, the former Jedi Naq Med.]]
During the [[New Republic Era]], [[Karr Nuq Sin]] inherited the Jedi robes of his [[Nuq Sin family|great-grandfather]], Padawan [[Naq Med]], who left the Jedi Order before the time of the Clone Wars. Inspired by his Jedi heritage, Nuq Sin endeavored to rediscover the lost history and relics of the Jedi Order.<ref name="Force Collector">''[[Force Collector]]''</ref>

After the Order's collapse, the Jedi were mythologized and [[Deity|deified]] as the subject of legends. These stories created a romanticized impression of the Jedi Knights garbed in brown robes<ref name="TLJ EE" /> and armed with "[[Lightsaber|laser swords]]."<ref name="The Children from Tehar">{{Resistance|The Children from Tehar}}</ref> [[Rose Tico]], a [[technician]] who served in the Resistance, referred to the [[Padawan braid]] as a "little [[rat]] tail" after [[Finn]] informed her about Rey's search for Skywalker.<ref name="TLJ EE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jedi garments first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']],<ref name="Episode IV" /> though they were not revealed to be the general wear of the Jedi until [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="Episode I" />
